body.title: [sprint] Resume-with-answer loop: inject the answer, push DS to the next wall
body.description:
**Problem:** disabling escalation alone makes DS re-hit the SAME first wall every pass — it does not 'fail further and further along'. The 'further' only happens if each harvested wall gets resolved and fed back: stuck → name the defeating question → the question is answered (tree / CC / Akien) → DS RESUMES with that answer injected → hits the NEXT wall, deeper in. This resume-with-answer loop IS the discovery engine; without it harvest-mode is a stall.

**Proposed shape:** given a recorded stuck point (ticket + transcript + defeating_question) and an ANSWER to that question, resume the harvest run with the answer injected into context and continue from the stall. The resumed run must reach a STRICTLY LATER wall, pinned to the TURN INDEX (integer M > N, where N is the recorded stall turn) so the progress test is crisp, not soft — or it logs 'no progress' (the answer didn't unstick it — itself a signal). Answer source is pluggable (manual/CC/tree); this ticket builds the inject+resume+progress-measurement mechanism, not the answer-provider.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/agentic_loop.py (resume-from-stall entry: seed context with the prior transcript + injected answer), architect_editor.py (thread resume), a progress-measure helper (turn/plan-position reached).

**Design rules:** OOP-first; log the resume + the progress delta (interface crossing); inference-proxy-only; the resume must be deterministic given (stuck point, answer) for testability.

**Scope boundary:** inject-answer + resume + progress-measurement. NOT the answer-provider, NOT auto-answering from the tree.

**Validation:** a resume with a valid injected answer reaches a later turn/progress marker than the recorded stall; a resume with a useless answer logs 'no progress'.

**Observable via:** log.info('AgenticLoop: resumed ticket=%s from turn=%d reached turn=%d progress_delta=%+d').

**Test plan:** hermetic — a stubbed device scripted to stall at turn N, then (given the injected answer in context) proceed to turn M>N. PROOF NODE: the resumed run reaches a strictly later progress marker than the recorded stall when the answer is injected, and reports 'no progress' when it is not — red (today: no resume path; a fresh run restarts at turn 0 and re-hits wall N) → green.

**audit-emphasis:** needs-deep-smell (L, stateful resume).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
